pages.raw_text:
WALLACE, Idaho, Jan. 23.-The
magnificent success of the Spokane
& Occur d'Alene electric railway has
inspired the thought of extending the
road through to Wallace, Capitalists
from the east and Spokane have been
looking over the route and early con-
struction is contemplated.


The proposed route runs from
Occur d'Alene through Fourth of July
canyon and by the old mission to
Wallace. This is the route selected
by the Northern Pacific railway in
1887. Most of the land along the
route is owned by the government
and the Northern Pacific Railway
company has long since lost its right
of way.


The electric road is to be built
closer TO the old Mullan road, mill
tary road between Fort Benton,
Mont, and Walla Walla, than the sur
vey of the Northern Pacific Railway
company.
